session_hint: the React SSR hydration mismatch investigation
question: What did we conclude was causing the React hydration mismatch errors?
turns: React is logging hydration mismatch errors in production. The root cause is clear from the first trace: the server and client both call Date.now() independently during the render of our CountdownBanner component. They produce different timestamps, so the rendered HTML diverges and React bails on reconciliation.
Investigated CSS-in-JS as a secondary suspect: we use styled-components with SSR enabled. Class name generation is deterministic and stable across server and client. Not the source of mismatches.
Checked lazy-loaded components — all are wrapped in Suspense with static string fallbacks. They render identically on both sides. Not contributing.

Event handler registration was briefly suspected because React 18 changed how event delegation works. But event handlers don't produce text content and can't cause the specific text-node mismatches we're seeing. Ruled out.
Confirmed by commenting out Date.now() in CountdownBanner and replacing with a static value: hydration errors disappear completely. Pages without any timestamp rendering hydrate cleanly.
Fix: pass the render timestamp as a prop from getServerSideProps so both server and client receive the same value. Applied in one place, no library changes needed.
Deployed. Hydration errors dropped to zero in Sentry within 10 minutes of rollout.
Added an ESLint rule to flag bare Date.now() and new Date() calls inside React component bodies.
CSS-in-JS class names and lazy-loaded Suspense components confirmed clean. Closing those investigation threads.
